name: Trehalose
summary: Trehalose is normalized for lysosomal and TFEB-linked preclinical evidence in Batten disease models.
mechanism_summary: Uses trehalose exposure to activate lysosomal/autophagy programs and test storage-burden and survival outcomes in disease models.

open_questions: Whether the linked evidence generalizes beyond the specific population, model, tissue, or disease context already recorded in the public finding. Linked context: Trehalose TFEB activation in Batten disease models; Trehalose activated TFEB and improved lysosomal storage-disease model outcomes.
Whether mechanism-specific effects can be separated from combination, product, delivery, or study-design confounding.
Whether future studies can connect the observed biomarker, functional, or model-organism signal to durable aging-relevant outcomes.
